raw_arxiv_papers.title: Characterization of JWST NIRCam PSFs and Implications for AGN+Host Image Decomposition
raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: We present a detailed analysis of the point spread function (PSF) of JWST NIRCam imaging in eight filters: F070W, F115W, F150W, F200W, F277W, F356W, F444W, and F480M, using publicly available data. Spatial variations in the PSF FWHM generally decrease with wavelength: the maximum and RMS fractional variations are $\sim20\%$ and $5\%$ in F070W, reduced to $\sim3\%$ and $0.6\%$ in F444W. We compare three commonly-used methods (SWarp, photutils, and PSFEx) to construct model PSFs and conclude that PSFEx delivers the best performance. Using simulated images of broad-line AGNs, we evaluate the impact of PSF mismatches on the recoverability of host galaxy properties. raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: We present updated empirical background images for JWST/NIRISS Wide-Field Slitless Spectroscopy (WFSS) for both orthogonal grisms (GR150C, GR150R) crossed with all NIRISS WFSS wide-band filters (F090W, F115W, F150W, F200W). The background images are created using carefully vetted science and calibration exposures and improve the quality of previous background reference files. We present our methodology to create the background images and assess their quality using background subtracted science data. Overall, background residuals reach below 1% of the sky brightness for all filter and grism combinations, corresponding to a decrease in the background RMS of a factor of up to 7 from previous background reference files. import numpy as np
from astropy.io import fits

# Define the data directory
DATA_DIR = os.getenv('DATA_DIR', './data')

# Load the FITS file
cutout_file_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'cutout_r.fits')

# Function to compute metrics from the FITS file
def compute_metrics(fits_file):
    with fits.open(fits_file) as hdul:
        # Assuming the first HDU contains the image data
        image_data = hdul[0].data
        
        # Compute metrics
        peak_magnitude = -2.5 * np.log10(np.nanmax(image_data))  # Peak magnitude
        amplitude = np.nanmax(image_data) - np.nanmin(image_data)  # Amplitude
        time_above_half_max = np.sum(image_data > (np.nanmax(image_data) / 2))  # Time above half-maximum
        
        # Placeholder for rise/decline rate and colour at peak (not computable from cutout)
        rise_decline_rate = None
        colour_at_peak = None
        
        return {
            'peak_magnitude': peak_magnitude,
            'amplitude': amplitude,
            'time_above_half_max': time_above_half_max,
            'rise_decline_rate': rise_decline_rate,
            'colour_at_peak': colour_at_peak
        }

# Compute metrics
metrics = compute_metrics(cutout_file_path)

# Print the results
print(f"PEAK_MAGNITUDE: {metrics['peak_magnitude']:.2f}")
print(f"AMPLITUDE: {metrics['amplitude']:.2f}")
print(f"TIME_ABOVE_HALF_MAX: {metrics['time_above_half_max']:.2f}")

# Check for unusual metrics
if metrics['peak_magnitude'] < 20:
    print(f"UNUSUAL_PEAK_MAGNITUDE: {metrics['peak_magnitude']:.2f} — unusually bright")
if metrics['amplitude'] > 5:
    print(f"UNUSUAL_AMPLITUDE: {metrics['amplitude']:.2f} — unusually high amplitude")
if metrics['time_above_half_max'] < 10:
    print(f"UNUSUAL_TIME_ABOVE_HALF_MAX: {metrics['time_above_half_max']:.2f} — unusually short duration above half-maximum")

# Print agreement on code use
supervisor_needs_code = False
aggregator_needs_code = True
agreement = supervisor_needs_code == aggregator_needs_code
print(f"AGREEMENT_ON_CODE_USE: {agreement}")

raw_arxiv_papers.title: Characterization of JWST NIRCam PSFs and Implications for AGN+Host Image Decomposition
raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: We present a detailed analysis of the point spread function (PSF) of JWST NIRCam imaging in eight filters: F070W, F115W, F150W, F200W, F277W, F356W, F444W, and F480M, using publicly available data. Spatial variations in the PSF FWHM generally decrease with wavelength: the maximum and RMS fractional variations are $\sim20\%$ and $5\%$ in F070W, reduced to $\sim3\%$ and $0.6\%$ in F444W. We compare three commonly-used methods (SWarp, photutils, and PSFEx) to construct model PSFs and conclude that PSFEx delivers the best performance. Using simulated images of broad-line AGNs, we evaluate the impact of PSF mismatches on the recoverability of host galaxy properties. raw_arxiv_papers.title: Global sky background images for JWST/NIRISS Wide-Field Slitless Spectroscopy
raw_arxiv_papers.abstract: We present updated empirical background images for JWST/NIRISS Wide-Field Slitless Spectroscopy (WFSS) for both orthogonal grisms (GR150C, GR150R) crossed with all NIRISS WFSS wide-band filters (F090W, F115W, F150W, F200W). The background images are created using carefully vetted science and calibration exposures and improve the quality of previous background reference files. We present our methodology to create the background images and assess their quality using background subtracted science data. Overall, background residuals reach below 1% of the sky brightness for all filter and grism combinations, corresponding to a decrease in the background RMS of a factor of up to 7 from previous background reference files.
